mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rates signal deteriorating labor market conditions that depress corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates robust supply-side activity that supports corporate profitability, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO rises > 1.0% in 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP and industrial production signals strong aggregate demand, driving equity valuations higher through improved earnings prospects and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and INDPRO both rise > 1.0% in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where both GDPC1 and INDPRO rise > 1.0% in 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening cycles, indicated by rising federal funds rates above 4.5%, compress equity valuations through higher discount rates, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts reflect elevated demand for residential construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST rises > 2.0% in 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation, indicated by rising CPI, erodes real returns and forces central bank rate hikes, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances despite CPIAUCSL rising > 0.5% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth signals a tightening labor market that increases wage pressures and corporate costs, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS rises > 100k in 1 month.
